note: Faiyum is the direct installed agricultural-hydraulic site anchor for a generic Roman-period irrigation proxy
Jerusalem is the plan-required historic-site anchor for the AD 1 Second Temple and its AD 70 destruction current
Ravenna is the direct installed city-point proxy for Augustus' 27 BC military harbour at Classe to the southeast
Mainz is the direct installed city-point anchor for Drusus' 13/12 BC Mogontiacum legionary camp opposite the Main
Neuss is the direct installed city-point anchor for the 16 BC Novaesium timber-and-earth Roman garrison at the Erft mouth
Nijmegen is the direct installed city-point anchor for the c.19 BC Augustan Hunnerberg military camp active around AD 1
Basel is the reviewed installed near-site anchor for the Kaiseraugst military camp beside Augusta Raurica after the Augustan 15 BC refoundation
Augsburg is the direct installed anchor for the Augsburg-Oberhausen military depot and timber camp around the AD 1 boundary
Strasbourg is the direct installed city-point anchor for Drusus' 12 BC Argentoratum legionary camp
Recklinghausen is the reviewed close installed anchor for Haltern's fortified Augustan main camp active around AD 1
Bonn is the direct installed city-point anchor for the early Augustan auxiliary camp established during Drusus' Rhine offensive before the later permanent legionary fortress
Wesel is the reviewed installed near-site anchor for the Augustan Vetera I legionary camp at Xanten active around AD 1
Sisak is the direct installed field for the recorded Augustan Siscia garrison while its precise early-first-century legionary station remains unsettled
